title: Market Brief — Saturday, May 2, 2026
summary: Compute is tightening fast across next-gen silicon. A100 sits in oversupply at 85% availability, but B200 is scarce at 25% and GB200 is in severe shortage at 10% with no reliable spot pricing yet. H200 availability is a thin 45%, with OCPI volatility screaming at 83.3% annualized, the biggest price signal in the stack, while H100 utilization hit 99.6%, up 8.6pp in 30 days despite 70% nominal availability.
paragraphs: Compute is tightening fast across next-gen silicon. A100 sits in oversupply at 85% availability, but B200 is scarce at 25% and GB200 is in severe shortage at 10% with no reliable spot pricing yet. H200 availability is a thin 45%, with OCPI volatility screaming at 83.3% annualized, the biggest price signal in the stack, while H100 utilization hit 99.6%, up 8.6pp in 30 days despite 70% nominal availability.

B200 is the only GPU where mid-tier inference turns profitable at 33% margin on Llama 70B. Every other chip loses money at medium pricing: MI300X is the worst, bleeding at negative 217% margin, meaning providers on AMD iron selling at $3/M output are losing roughly $8.90 per million tokens against TCO-adjusted cost of $11.90/M. Provider grades: DeepSeek Burning Cash, OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong on premium tiers, Google Neutral, xAI Burning Cash.

Overvalued: OpenAI o3 Pro at $80/M output is 160x the cost of DeepSeek V3.2 with no benchmark justification for that spread. Undervalued: B200 spot at $4.20/hr against OCPI of $5.03/hr represents an 20% discount to trade-clearing price, buy B200 capacity now before utilization, already up 35.9pp this month, closes the gap.
bullets: 25% availability on B200 and 10% on GB200 signal severe scarcity in next-gen compute.
99.6% utilization on H100 marks effectively zero spare capacity despite 70% nominal availability.
$0.378 per million output tokens from DeepSeek V3.2 is the market floor, 99.4% below GPT-4 launch pricing.
83.3% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI is the highest across all GPU classes.
$11.90 TCO-adjusted cost per million tokens on MI300X makes AMD the most expensive inference platform.
21.41 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token pricing has fallen 78.6% from the GPT-4 launch baseline.
